pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies survey title: 'Framing Effect Experiment - Cognitive Decision Making Study 2024'. The keyword 'Framing Effect' is present in this title.

Task description explicitly lists exactly 5 question groups in order: (1) Informed Consent and Instructions, (2) Scenario A - Disease Outbreak, (3) Scenario B - Financial Decision, (4) Scenario C - Environmental Policy, (5) Demographics and Debrief

Task description explicitly states: 'Set the RANDOMIZATION GROUP for the three Scenario groups (A, B, C) to the value: experimental'. Verifier.py confirms this check at line 103: most_common_rg.lower() == "experimental"

Task description explicitly states that Scenario A, B, and C groups (3 groups) are marked as 'randomized'. Instructions state: 'All three must share the same randomization group name so LimeSurvey randomizes their presentation order.'

Task description explicitly states that 'Informed Consent and Instructions' (always shown first — NO randomization) and 'Demographics and Debrief' (always shown last — NO randomization) are the 2 fixed groups. Task states: 'The Informed Consent and Demographics groups must NOT have any randomization group set.'

Web search confirmed Tversky & Kahneman published 'The Framing of Decisions and the Psychology of Choice' in Science 211(4481), January 30, 1981. The task description explicitly states: 'You are a psychology professor setting up an online replication of the classic Tversky & Kahneman (1981) framing effect experiment'

pi_items.metadata_value: Imagine an unusual disease is expected to kill 600 people with Program A: 200 saved; Program B: 1/3 chance all saved
pi_items.source: Web search of original Tversky & Kahneman 1981 paper confirmed: 'Imagine that the US is preparing for the outbreak of an unusual Asian disease, which is expected to kill 600 people' with program options matching task description (Program A saves 200 people, Program B has 1/3 probability that 600 people will be saved)

Task instruction states: 'DISABLE backward navigation (participants cannot return to previous pages — critical for experimental validity)'. Verifier.py checks this at line 129: allowprev == 'N' awards 10 points
Task instruction states: 'ENABLE anonymized responses'. Verifier.py checks this at line 136: anonymized == 'Y' awards 10 points

pi_items.metadata_value: At least one question required for each group
pi_items.verified_value: At least 5 total questions required (1 per group minimum)
pi_items.source: Task states: 'Add at least one question to EACH of the 5 groups'. Verifier.py checks at line 120: q_count >= 5 awards 15 points

privileged_info_summary: This task is a faithful replication of the classic Tversky & Kahneman (1981) framing effect experiment from Science 211(4481). The core disease outbreak scenario (600 people at risk, Program A saves 200, Program B has 1/3 chance to save all) exactly matches the original 1981 paper's 'Asian disease problem.' The survey requires exactly 5 question groups with 3 randomized scenario groups (A, B, C) all sharing the randomization group name 'experimental', and 2 fixed groups (Informed Consent first and Demographics last) with no randomization. Critical experimental design features include disabled backward navigation and enabled anonymous responses. All metadata claims (title keyword, group count, randomization configuration, fixed group count) are accurate and verifiable through the task description and LimeSurvey database schema.
